skill.name: hyperframes
skill.description: Create video compositions, animations, title cards, overlays, captions, voiceovers, audio-reactive visuals, and scene transitions in HyperFrames HTML. Use when asked to build any HTML-based video content, add captions or subtitles synced to audio, generate text-to-speech narration, create audio-reactive animation (beat sync, glow, pulse driven by music), add animated text highlighting (marker sweeps, hand-drawn circles, burst lines, scribble, sketchout), or add transitions between scenes (crossfades, wipes, reveals, shader transitions). Covers composition authoring, timing, media, and the full video production workflow. For CLI commands (init, lint, preview, render, transcribe, tts) see the hyperframes-cli skill.
skill.summary: HTML-based video composition framework for creating animations, captions, voiceovers, and transitions
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 791 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate HTML-based video composition framework with documented CLI commands, file operations for composition authoring, and optional API integrations for transcription/TTS services. The detected patterns are benign: hex color codes in palette files (misidentified as Ruby commands), relative file paths (../) in documentation, and CLI tool invocations that are expected behavior for a video production tool.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Usage (Expected Behavior)
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run CLI commands like `npx hyperframes lint`, `npx hyperframes inspect`, and `node scripts/animation-map.mjs` for video composition validation. These are documented authoring tools, not command injection vulnerabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: CLI commands are explicitly documented in the skill as authoring tools. No user input is passed to shell execution. This is expected behavior for a video production framework.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Documentation for External APIs
low_findings.description: The transcript-guide.md shows curl examples with `$OPENAI_API_KEY` and `$GROQ_API_KEY` placeholders. These are documentation examples showing users how to authenticate with external transcription APIs - not actual credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variables are documented placeholders in instructional examples. Users are expected to provide their own API keys. No credentials are transmitted to third parties without user consent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hex Color Codes (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged hex color codes like `#FFBE0B`, `#FB5607` as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are legitimate CSS color values in palette reference files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Pattern '#FFBE0B' is standard CSS hex color syntax. The files contain only color palette definitions with no executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path References in Documentation (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer flagged `../` sequences as 'path traversal'. These are legitimate relative path references in markdown documentation for linking to sibling files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The '..' patterns are used in markdown relative links and file path descriptions within documentation, not in actual file system operations that could be exploited.

dangerous_patterns.title: Word Count Commands in Validation Instructions (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer flagged word count commands as 'system reconnaissance'. These are documented CLI commands users run to validate their compositions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents that users should run validation commands like `hyperframes inspect` and `hyperframes validate`. This is intended authoring workflow, not reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Create HTML-based video compositions and animations
content.value_statement: Creating video animations requires learning specialized video production software and complex rendering pipelines. HyperFrames lets you write HTML to define video compositions, use GSAP for animations, and render professional videos with captions, transitions, and audio.
actual_capabilities: Create video compositions using HTML with data-* attributes for timing and GSAP for animation
Add synchronized captions and subtitles with word-level timing from transcripts
Generate text-to-speech narration using local Kokoro-ONNX models
Create audio-reactive animations that respond to music beats, glow, and pulse effects
Add scene transitions including crossfades, wipes, reveals, and shader effects
Run validation checks for layout, contrast (WCAG AA), and animation choreography
limitations: Cannot render video without the hyperframes CLI and render engine dependencies
Does not include video recording or webcam capture capabilities
Requires user to provide audio files for transcription and TTS features

use_cases.title: Marketing and social media videos
use_cases.description: Create animated title cards, product showcases, and promotional content with brand-consistent colors and professional transitions.

use_cases.title: Educational and explainer content
use_cases.description: Build animated explainer videos with captions, voiceovers, and visual highlights that draw attention to key points.

use_cases.title: Event and conference presentations
use_cases.description: Generate animated slides and highlight reels with smooth transitions, typography effects, and audio-reactive elements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic composition request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HyperFrames composition with a title card that says [TITLE] on a dark background with the text fading in. Use the clean-corporate palette.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple animated title card

prompt_templates.title: Captioned video request
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an audio file at [PATH]. Create a HyperFrames composition with word-level captions synced to the audio. Use a dark background with white text.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need subtitles for an audio file

prompt_templates.title: Transition sequence request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-scene transition sequence using crossfade, blur, and page burn transitions between scenes. Apply the bold-energetic color palette.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need professional scene transitions

prompt_templates.title: Full video production request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete HyperFrames video with: title card, 3 content scenes with captions, audio-reactive background animations, and smooth transitions. Style should be [STYLE].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a complete video from start to finish

output_examples.input: Create a simple title card with "Welcome" text
output_examples.output: An HTML composition with static positioning and GSAP entrance animation that fades in the title text.

output_examples.input: Add captions synced to my audio file
output_examples.output: A composition with word-level caption elements and timing data derived from the transcript.

output_examples.input: Create a crossfade between two scenes
output_examples.output: A two-scene composition with GSAP-driven opacity transitions for smooth visual continuity.

best_practices: Define a visual identity (DESIGN.md or visual-style.md) before writing any composition HTML
Build the static layout first, positioning elements where they appear at their most visible moment
Run `hyperframes validate` to check WCAG contrast and `hyperframes inspect` to verify layout before rendering
Use GSAP.from() for entrances and GSAP.to() for exits, animating FROM/TO the CSS ground truth
anti_patterns: Do not write compositions with generic or default colors - always apply a defined visual identity
Do not position scene content containers with absolute positioning and hardcoded dimensions
Do not skip layout and contrast validation before final rendering

faq.question: What is HyperFrames?
faq.answer: HyperFrames is an HTML-based video composition framework. You write HTML with data-* attributes for timing and CSS for styling. GSAP handles animation. The framework manages clip visibility, media playback, and timeline sync.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the hyperframes CLI installed via npm and the rendering engine (Puppeteer and FFmpeg) for video output. Run `npx hyperframes init` to set up a project.

faq.question: Can I add captions from an audio file?
faq.answer: Yes. You can use external transcription APIs (OpenAI Whisper, Groq) or local transcription. The skill converts timestamps to word-level caption elements synced to your audio.

faq.question: How do I add voiceover narration?
faq.answer: Use the built-in TTS command with Kokoro-ONNX models. Run `npx hyperframes tts` with your script text. The skill supports voice selection and generates audio files.

faq.question: What animation capabilities are available?
faq.answer: GSAP powers all animations including entrances, exits, text effects, and audio-reactive behaviors. You can create beat-synced animations, glow effects, and complex choreography using the animation map tool.

faq.question: How do I validate my composition before rendering?
faq.answer: Run `npx hyperframes lint` for syntax checks, `npx hyperframes validate` for WCAG contrast, and `npx hyperframes inspect` for layout verification. Fix all warnings before rendering.
